hypotheses.title: Dithiolane-Chalcopyrite Ligand Homology: Lipoic Acid as Molecular Fossil of Vent Cu-S Coordination
hypotheses.bridge: Structural homology between biological dithiolane and vent thiol-Cu coordination chemistry
hypotheses.key_claim: Lipoic acid dithiolane ring is electronically homologous to vent thiol-Cu complexes; KD ~10^-17 reflects evolutionary optimization

hypotheses.title: FDX1 Redox Potential Tuned to Alkaline Vent Cu2+/Cu+ Boundary
hypotheses.bridge: Quantitative Eh-pH correspondence between vent geochemistry and mitochondrial copper redox
hypotheses.key_claim: Ligand-extended Pourbaix diagram shows Cu2+/Cu+ boundary at -260 to -300 mV, coinciding with FDX1 midpoint

hypotheses.title: H2S Potentiates Cuproptosis Through CuS Nanoparticle Formation and pH-Dependent Re-release
hypotheses.bridge: CuS precipitation/dissolution cycle shared between vent chimneys and mitochondria
hypotheses.key_claim: CuS nanoparticles form in mitochondria with H2S; oxidative dissolution releases Cu2+ in feed-forward loop

hypotheses.title: Fe-S Cluster Cannibalization During Cuproptosis Recapitulates Geochemical Cu-Fe Replacement Series
hypotheses.bridge: Cu-Fe sulfide replacement chemistry shared between vent mineralogy and Fe-S cluster biochemistry
hypotheses.key_claim: Fe-S cluster loss is direct Cu+-for-Fe2+ displacement with 29 orders of magnitude Ksp driving force

hypotheses.title: Mitochondrial CuL Is a Dithiolane-Containing Metabolite Predicted by Vent Thiol Speciation
hypotheses.bridge: Vent thiol speciation as predictive framework for CuL identity
hypotheses.key_claim: CuL is a low-MW dithiolane/vicinal dithiol with Cu+ log K = 14-16

hypotheses.title: CuS-H2O2 Fenton-Analog Cycle Generates Hydroxyl Radical Bursts During Cuproptosis
hypotheses.key_claim: CuS + H2O2 -> catalytic HO radical cycle localized to mitochondrial CuS deposits

hypotheses.title: Evolutionary Reconstruction: Alkaline Vent Cu Gradients Selected for FDX1-LIAS-Lipoylation Axis
hypotheses.bridge: Evolutionary continuity: vent copper homeostasis as selection pressure
hypotheses.key_claim: FDX1-LIAS axis evolved in alkaline vents for copper handling; cuproptosis is this system collapsing when overwhelmed
